content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, let SSS denote the set of integers from 111 to NNN (inclusive).

You are given a function f:S×S→Sf : S \times S \rightarrow Sf:S×S→S, such that f(x,y)=f(y,x)f(x, y) = f(y, x)f(x,y)=f(y,x).

For an array AAA, we define it's value as following:


The score of a subarray (of size at least 222) is defined as :

let M1M_1M1​ be the maximum element in the subarray, and M2M_2M2​ be the second maximum element in the subarray.
The score is f(M1,M2)f(M_1, M_2)f(M1​,M2​).
It is possible that M1=M2M_1 = M_2M1​=M2​ if the maximum occurs at least twice.


The value of an array is defined as the maximum score over all possible subarrays of size at least 222.
There are NNN^NNN possible arrays satisfying


∣A∣=N|A| = N∣A∣=N
1≤Ai≤N1 \le A_i \le N1≤Ai​≤N.
Find the sum of their values modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The iii-th of the next NNN lines contains NNN integers each - f(i,1),f(i,2),…,f(i,N)f(i, 1), f(i, 2), \ldots, f(i, N)f(i,1),f(i,2),…,f(i,N).




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of values over all arrays modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of 2N2^N2N does not exceed 2162^{16}216.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 444 arrays : [1,1],[2,2],[2,1],[1,2][1, 1], [2, 2], [2, 1], [1, 2][1,1],[2,2],[2,1],[1,2]. The first and the second have their values as 111, while the third and fourth have a value of 222.
Test Case 2 : Only [1,1][1, 1][1,1] has it's value as 111. The others have a value of 222.
